content: We can frame a two-layered autonomous self-improvement architecture for LLMs where Self-Refine acts as the iterative "tactical correction" layer for immediate output polishing, and Reflexion acts as the "strategic memory" layer that accumulates generalized lessons from past Self-Refine cycles to improve future refinement attempts across unrelated tasks. This turns isolated output refinement into cumulative, agent-like self-improvement without any additional supervised training or external reward signals.
